audit_status.source_base: Hebrew Exodus / Shemot text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for Passover/Pesach, unleavened bread, firstborn judgment, mixed multitude, memorial, and liberation night.

verses.restored: YHWH said to Moses and Aaron in land of Egypt, saying,
This month shall be for you beginning of months; it shall be first for you of months of year.
Speak to all congregation of Israel, saying: On tenth of this month each shall take for themselves a lamb by fathers' house, a lamb for household.
If the household is too small for a lamb, then one and neighbor near one's house shall take according to number of souls/lives; according to each one's eating you shall count for the lamb.
Your lamb shall be complete, male, son of a year; from sheep or goats you may take it.
It shall be for you under guard until fourteenth day of this month, and all assembly of congregation of Israel shall slaughter it between the evenings.
They shall take from the blood/life and put on two doorposts and lintel upon houses in which they eat it.
They shall eat the flesh in that night, roasted with fire, with unleavened bread and bitter herbs they shall eat it.
Do not eat from it raw or boiled in water, but roasted with fire, its head with its legs and its inward parts.
You shall not leave from it until morning; what remains from it until morning you shall burn with fire.
Thus you shall eat it: your hips girded, your sandals on your feet, your staff in your hand; you shall eat it in haste. It is Pesach/Passover to YHWH.
I will pass through land of Egypt in this night and strike every firstborn in land of Egypt, from human to beast; and upon all gods of Egypt I will do judgments. I am YHWH.
The blood/life shall be for you a sign upon houses where you are; I will see the blood/life and pass over you, and plague shall not be upon you for destruction when I strike land of Egypt.
This day shall be for you memorial, and you shall celebrate it as festival to YHWH; throughout your generations, as age-long statute you shall celebrate it.

Seven days you shall eat unleavened bread. Surely on first day you shall remove leaven from your houses; for everyone eating leavened bread from first day until seventh day, that soul/life shall be cut off from Israel.
On first day there shall be holy calling/assembly, and on seventh day holy calling/assembly for you; no work shall be done in them, only what every soul/life eats, that alone may be done by you.
You shall keep unleavened bread, because on this very day I brought your hosts out from land of Egypt; you shall keep this day throughout your generations as age-long statute.
In first month, on fourteenth day of month in evening, you shall eat unleavened bread until twenty-first day of month in evening.
Seven days leaven shall not be found in your houses; for everyone eating what is leavened, that soul/life shall be cut off from congregation of Israel, among sojourner and native of land.
You shall eat nothing leavened; in all your dwellings you shall eat unleavened bread.
Moses called all elders of Israel and said to them, Draw out and take flock-animals for yourselves according to your clans, and slaughter the Pesach/Passover.
Take bundle of hyssop, dip in blood/life that is in basin, and touch lintel and two doorposts with blood/life in basin; none of you shall go out from opening of one's house until morning.
YHWH will pass through to strike Egypt; he will see blood/life on lintel and two doorposts, and YHWH will pass over opening and will not allow the destroyer to enter your houses to strike.
You shall keep this matter as statute for you and your children into the age.
When you come to the land that YHWH will give you, as he spoke, you shall keep this service.
And when your children say to you, What is this service to you?
you shall say, It is sacrifice of Pesach/Passover to YHWH, who passed over houses of children of Israel in Egypt when he struck Egypt and rescued our houses. The people bowed low and worshiped.
The children of Israel went and did; as YHWH commanded Moses and Aaron, so they did.
It happened at midnight: YHWH struck every firstborn in land of Egypt, from firstborn of Pharaoh sitting on his throne to firstborn of captive who was in house of pit, and every firstborn of beast.
Pharaoh rose in the night, he and all his servants and all Egypt, and there was great cry in Egypt, for there was no house where there was not dead.
He called Moses and Aaron by night and said, Rise, go out from midst of my people, both you and children of Israel; go serve/worship YHWH according to your word.
Also take your flock and herd, as you spoke, and go; and bless me also.
Egypt pressed upon the people, to hurry to send them from the land, for they said, All of us are dead ones.
The people lifted their dough before it was leavened, their kneading bowls bound in their garments upon their shoulders.
The children of Israel did according to word of Moses and asked from Egypt objects of silver, objects of gold, and garments.
YHWH gave the people favor in eyes of Egypt, and they let them have what they asked; and they plundered Egypt.
The children of Israel journeyed from Rameses to Succoth, about six hundred thousand footmen, the men apart from little ones.
And a mixed multitude also went up with them, and flock and herd, very heavy livestock.

They baked the dough they brought out from Egypt into unleavened cakes, for it was not leavened, because they were driven out from Egypt and could not delay, and also they had not made provisions for themselves.
The dwelling of children of Israel, which they dwelt in Egypt, was four hundred thirty years.
It happened at end of four hundred thirty years, on this very day, all hosts of YHWH went out from land of Egypt.
It is night of keeping watch to YHWH for bringing them out from land of Egypt; this night belongs to YHWH, keeping watch by all children of Israel throughout generations.
YHWH said to Moses and Aaron, This is statute of Pesach/Passover: no foreign son shall eat of it.
But every servant bought with silver, when you circumcise him, then he may eat of it.
A settler and hired worker shall not eat of it.
In one house it shall be eaten; you shall not bring out from house any of flesh outside, and bone you shall not break in it.
All congregation of Israel shall do it.

When a sojourner sojourns with you and would make Pesach/Passover to YHWH, let every male of his be circumcised; then he may come near to do it, and he shall be like native of land. But no uncircumcised one shall eat of it.
One instruction/Torah shall be for native and for sojourner sojourning in your midst.
All children of Israel did as YHWH commanded Moses and Aaron; so they did.
It happened on this very day: YHWH brought children of Israel out from land of Egypt according to their hosts.

translation_notes: Pesach/Passover is preserved with Hebrew term and explained through passing-over/protection and liberation night.
The calendar is reoriented around liberation; time begins again for the freed people.
Mixed multitude in v.38 is crucial: liberation includes non-Israelites joining the exodus.
One Torah/instruction for native and sojourner in v.49 should be preserved as covenant inclusion with boundary markers.
Plunder language represents transfer from oppressor wealth after forced labor; avoid glamorizing theft detached from oppression context.
safety_note: This chapter contains the death of firstborn and must be handled with solemnity. Keep it tethered to Pharaoh's death-policy, repeated hardening, and liberation from systemic oppression. Do not generalize into violence theology or anti-Egyptian hatred.
